workflow_metadata.description: Comprehensive release validation workflow for HDIM platform with 5 phases and 21 validation tasks
workflow_metadata.author: HDIM Platform Team

phases.name: Code Quality & Testing Validation
phases.description: Validate codebase quality, test coverage, schema synchronization, and HIPAA compliance

tasks.name: Entity-Migration Synchronization Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate that all JPA entities have corresponding Liquibase migrations across all 34 microservices.

**Actions:**
1. Run EntityMigrationValidationTest for each service
2. Verify 100% pass rate (0 failures)
3. Check that ddl-auto is set to 'validate' in all application*.yml files (NEVER create/update)
4. Generate entity-migration-report.md in docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/

**Completion Criteria:**
- All EntityMigrationValidationTest tests pass
- ddl-auto: validate confirmed in all 34 services
- No schema-validation errors in logs
- All entities have synchronized Liquibase migrations

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/test-entity-migration-sync.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/entity-migration-report.md
completion_criteria: ddl-auto: validate confirmed in all 34 services
All entities have synchronized Liquibase migrations

tasks.name: Liquibase Rollback SQL Coverage Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate that all Liquibase changesets across all 34 services have explicit <rollback> tags.

**Actions:**
1. Run backend/scripts/test-liquibase-rollback.sh
2. Verify 100% coverage (199/199 changesets as of Phase 21)
3. Check all changesets have explicit <rollback> tags (not auto-generated)
4. Generate liquibase-rollback-report.md

**Completion Criteria:**
- test-liquibase-rollback.sh exits with code 0
- 100% rollback SQL coverage confirmed
- No changesets missing <rollback> tags
- Rollback patterns validated against DATABASE_MIGRATION_RUNBOOK.md

**Validation Command:**
./backend/scripts/test-liquibase-rollback.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/liquibase-rollback-report.md

tasks.name: Test Suite Execution and Coverage Validation
tasks.prompt:
Run full Gradle test suite across all backend modules and validate test pass rate and coverage.

**Actions:**
1. Run ./gradlew test across all backend modules
2. Validate test pass rate is 100% (1,577/1,577 tests as of Phase 21)
3. Check service layer coverage ≥80% and overall coverage ≥70%
4. Generate JaCoCo coverage reports
5. Generate test-coverage-report.md with metrics

**Completion Criteria:**
- ./gradlew test exits with BUILD SUCCESSFUL
- Test pass rate: 100% (0 failures, 0 errors)
- Service layer coverage ≥80%
- Overall coverage ≥70%
- JaCoCo reports generated

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/run-full-test-suite.sh

**Expected Outputs:**
- backend/build/reports/tests/test/index.html
- backend/build/reports/jacoco/test/html/index.html
- docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/test-coverage-report.md

tasks.name: HIPAA Compliance Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate HIPAA compliance across all services for PHI handling and cache controls.

**Actions:**
1. Verify all @Cacheable PHI caches have TTL ≤5 minutes (300,000ms)
2. Confirm Cache-Control: no-store headers on all PHI endpoints
3. Validate @Audited annotations on PHI access methods
4. Check multi-tenant isolation tests exist and pass
5. Verify HIPAA-CACHE-COMPLIANCE.md requirements met
6. Generate HIPAA_COMPLIANCE_REPORT.md

**Completion Criteria:**
- All PHI caches have TTL ≤300,000ms (recommend 120,000ms)
- All PHI responses include Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache headers
- @Audited annotations present on PHI access methods
- Multi-tenant isolation tests passing
- HIPAA-CACHE-COMPLIANCE.md requirements verified

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-hipaa-compliance.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/HIPAA_COMPLIANCE_REPORT.md
completion_criteria: All PHI caches have TTL ≤300,000ms (5 minutes)
All PHI responses include Cache-Control: no-store
@Audited annotations present on PHI access methods

phases.name: Documentation Completeness Validation
phases.description: Validate all release documentation is complete, accurate, and production-ready

tasks.name: Release Notes Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate RELEASE_NOTES_v{VERSION}.md file exists and is complete.

**Actions:**
1. Check if docs/releases/v{VERSION}/RELEASE_NOTES_v{VERSION}.md exists
2. If not, auto-generate from template using generate-release-docs.sh
3. Validate content includes:
   - All new features with API endpoint documentation
   - Breaking changes clearly listed
   - Security/HIPAA enhancements
   - Performance improvements with metrics
   - Database migration summary (number of changesets, impacted services)
   - Known issues and workarounds

**Completion Criteria:**
- RELEASE_NOTES_v{VERSION}.md exists
- All new features documented with API examples
- Breaking changes section complete
- Security enhancements listed
- Database migration count accurate

**Auto-Generation:**
If file missing: ./scripts/release-validation/generate-release-docs.sh v{VERSION}

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/RELEASE_NOTES_v{VERSION}.md
completion_criteria: All new features documented with endpoints

tasks.name: Upgrade Guide Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate UPGRADE_GUIDE_v{VERSION}.md file exists and contains step-by-step upgrade instructions.

**Actions:**
1. Check if docs/releases/v{VERSION}/UPGRADE_GUIDE_v{VERSION}.md exists
2. If not, auto-generate from template
3. Validate content includes:
   - Step-by-step upgrade instructions (sequential, testable)
   - Required environment variable changes
   - Database migration steps (backup, migrate, verify)
   - Breaking change migration paths
   - Rollback procedures with commands
   - Service restart order (dependencies first)

**Completion Criteria:**
- UPGRADE_GUIDE_v{VERSION}.md exists
- Upgrade steps are sequential and testable
- Environment variable changes documented
- Database migration instructions present
- Rollback procedure documented with commands

**Auto-Generation:**
If file missing: ./scripts/release-validation/generate-release-docs.sh v{VERSION}

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/UPGRADE_GUIDE_v{VERSION}.md
completion_criteria: Upgrade steps are sequential and executable

tasks.name: CHANGELOG Update Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate CHANGELOG.md has been updated with v{VERSION} entry.

**Actions:**
1. Check if CHANGELOG.md (root directory) has v{VERSION} entry
2. Verify entry follows Keep a Changelog format
3. Validate all categories populated:
   - Added (new features)
   - Changed (modifications to existing functionality)
   - Deprecated (soon-to-be-removed features)
   - Removed (deleted features)
   - Fixed (bug fixes)
   - Security (security updates, HIPAA compliance)
4. Verify links to relevant PRs or issues included
5. Confirm release date is accurate

**Completion Criteria:**
- CHANGELOG.md contains v{VERSION} entry
- Entry follows Keep a Changelog format
- All categories populated (Added, Changed, Fixed, Security)
- Links to PRs/issues included
- Release date accurate

**Expected Output:**
CHANGELOG.md (updated)
completion_criteria: Entry follows Keep a Changelog format

tasks.name: API Documentation Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate OpenAPI specifications are generated for all modified services.

**Actions:**
1. Run docs/api/generate-openapi-specs.sh to generate specs
2. Verify Swagger UI is accessible for each service: /{service}/swagger-ui.html
3. Check that all new endpoints are documented with:
   - Request schemas (all parameters, body)
   - Response schemas (success + error responses)
   - Authentication requirements (@PreAuthorize roles)
   - Example requests and responses
4. Generate API documentation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- generate-openapi-specs.sh exits successfully
- OpenAPI specs generated for all modified services
- Swagger UI accessible at /{service}/swagger-ui.html
- All new endpoints have complete documentation
- API documentation report created

**Validation Command:**
./docs/api/generate-openapi-specs.sh

**Expected Outputs:**
- docs/api/openapi-specs/{service}-openapi.json (for each service)
- docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/api-documentation-report.md
completion_criteria: OpenAPI specs generated for modified services
Swagger UI accessible for each service

phases.name: Integration & Configuration Validation
phases.description: Validate service integration, distributed tracing, database connection pooling, Kafka messaging, and gateway authentication

tasks.name: Distributed Tracing OTLP Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate OpenTelemetry OTLP configuration across all 34 services.

**Actions:**
1. Verify OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT points to http://jaeger:4318/v1/traces in all services
2. Confirm OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL: http/protobuf in all services
3. Check _JAVA_OPTIONS includes -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true
4. Start services and validate Jaeger UI shows traces from ≥6 services
5. Test W3C Trace Context propagation across services
6. Generate Jaeger integration validation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All 34 services have complete OTLP environment variables
- OTLP endpoints correct (http://jaeger:4318/v1/traces)
- Jaeger UI accessible at http://localhost:16686
- ≥6 services sending traces to Jaeger confirmed
- W3C Trace Context propagation working

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-jaeger-integration.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/JAEGER_INTEGRATION_VALIDATION.md
completion_criteria: All 34 services have complete OTLP config

tasks.name: HikariCP Connection Pool Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate HikariCP configuration across all 34 services follows timing formula.

**Actions:**
1. Verify timing formula: max-lifetime ≥ 6x idle-timeout (1800s ≥ 6×300s)
2. Confirm traffic tier pool sizes:
   - HIGH traffic: maximum-pool-size=50
   - MEDIUM traffic: maximum-pool-size=20
   - LOW traffic: maximum-pool-size=10
3. Validate keepalive-time=240s (< idle-timeout)
4. Check leak-detection-threshold=60000 (60 seconds)
5. Verify no connection pool errors in service logs
6. Generate HikariCP configuration report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All 34 services pass HikariCP timing formula
- Traffic tier pool sizes correctly configured
- Keepalive-time=240s in all services
- Leak detection configured with 60s threshold
- No connection pool errors in logs

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-hikaricp-config.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/hikaricp-config-report.md
completion_criteria: All services pass timing formula (max-lifetime ≥ 6x idle-timeout)
Traffic tier pool sizes correct (HIGH=50, MEDIUM=20, LOW=10)

tasks.name: Kafka Trace Propagation Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate Kafka trace propagation across all 19 Kafka-enabled services.

**Actions:**
1. Verify spring.json.add.type.headers: false in all producers
2. Confirm spring.json.use.type.headers: false in all consumers
3. Check interceptor.classes configured:
   - Producer: KafkaProducerTraceInterceptor
   - Consumer: KafkaConsumerTraceInterceptor
4. Validate trace continuity across Kafka topics (send event, verify trace ID propagates)
5. Check for ClassNotFoundException errors in logs (indicates type header issues)
6. Generate Kafka tracing validation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All 19 Kafka services have type headers disabled
- KafkaProducerTraceInterceptor configured in all producers
- KafkaConsumerTraceInterceptor configured in all consumers
- Trace propagation working across Kafka topics
- No ClassNotFoundException errors in logs

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-kafka-tracing.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/kafka-tracing-report.md
completion_criteria: All 19 Kafka services have type headers disabled
Producer interceptor configured in all producers
Consumer interceptor configured in all consumers

tasks.name: Gateway Trust Authentication Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate gateway trust authentication pattern across all backend services.

**Actions:**
1. Verify SecurityConfig uses TrustedHeaderAuthFilter + TrustedTenantAccessFilter
2. Confirm filter ordering (TrustedHeaderAuthFilter BEFORE UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter)
3. Check all endpoints have @PreAuthorize annotations (scan all @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping)
4. Validate tests use GatewayTrustTestHeaders for authentication
5. Verify no JWT validation in backend services (only gateway validates JWT)
6. Generate gateway trust authentication validation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All backend services use TrustedHeaderAuthFilter
- Filter ordering correct in all SecurityConfig classes
- All API endpoints have @PreAuthorize annotations
- Integration tests use GatewayTrustTestHeaders
- No JWT validation in backend (gateway-only)

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-gateway-trust-auth.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/GATEWAY_TRUST_VALIDATION.md
completion_criteria: Filter ordering verified in all SecurityConfig
No JWT validation in backend services

phases.name: Deployment Readiness Validation
phases.description: Validate Docker configuration, health checks, environment variables, and deployment artifacts

tasks.name: Docker Image Build Validation
tasks.prompt:
Build all Docker images for modified services and validate security and optimization.

**Actions:**
1. Run docker compose build for all modified services
2. Validate all images use non-root user (UID 1001)
3. Check JVM container optimization flags present:
   - -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0
   - -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true
4. Verify image sizes are reasonable (<500MB for Java services)
5. Run security scan on images (check for vulnerabilities in base images)
6. Generate docker build validation report and image manifest

**Completion Criteria:**
- docker compose build completes successfully
- All images use non-root user (UID 1001)
- JVM optimization flags present in all Java services
- Image sizes documented in manifest
- No critical security vulnerabilities in base images

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/build-and-validate-images.sh

**Expected Outputs:**
- docs/releases/v{VERSION}/artifacts/docker-image-manifest.json
- docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/docker-build-report.md
- docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/security-scan-report.md
completion_criteria: No critical vulnerabilities in base images

tasks.name: Health Check Configuration Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate health check configuration in docker-compose.yml for all services.

**Actions:**
1. Verify all services have health check configuration
2. Check health check timing parameters are appropriate:
   - interval: 30s (standard) or service-specific
   - timeout: 10s
   - retries: 3
   - start-period: 60s (or longer for slow-starting services)
3. Test all health endpoints return HTTP 200 when healthy
4. Validate service dependencies use condition: service_healthy
5. Verify services start in correct order (dependencies first)
6. Generate health check validation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All services have health check configuration
- Health check timing parameters appropriate
- All health endpoints return HTTP 200
- Service dependencies use condition: service_healthy
- Services start in correct order

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-health-checks.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/health-check-validation-report.md
completion_criteria: All services have health check config
Timing params: interval=30s, timeout=10s, retries=3, start-period=60s+

tasks.name: Environment Variable Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate all required environment variables are documented and configured correctly.

**Actions:**
1. Check PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST for complete env var list
2. Verify sensitive vars use ${SECRET} placeholders in docker-compose.yml (no hardcoded secrets)
3. Confirm all OTLP environment variables present for distributed tracing
4. Validate database connection vars correct for all 34 services
5. Check Kafka bootstrap servers configured correctly
6. Verify environment-specific profiles (dev/staging/prod) documented
7. Generate environment variable validation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- All required env vars documented in deployment checklist
- No hardcoded secrets in docker-compose.yml
- OTLP environment variables complete for all services
- Database connection vars correct
- Kafka bootstrap servers configured

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-environment-vars.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/environment-validation-report.md
completion_criteria: All required env vars in PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST
Database vars correct for all 34 services

tasks.name: CX Access Admin Endpoint Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate CX access-admin endpoints are reachable and properly protected in the target deployment.

**Actions:**
1. Set SERVICE_URL to target API endpoint
2. Run ./scripts/release-validation/validate-cx-access-admin.sh v{VERSION}
3. Verify unauthenticated requests to:
   - /api/admin/customer-access
   - /api/admin/identity-access
   are rejected (401/403)
4. If admin token available, set ADMIN_BEARER_TOKEN and re-run
5. If safe in environment, set ALLOW_WRITE=true and verify create/delete smoke path
6. Review generated report

**Completion Criteria:**
- /health and /api/auth/config return 200
- Unauthenticated admin endpoint access is rejected
- Authenticated admin list checks pass when token provided
- Optional write-path smoke check passes when enabled

**Validation Command:**
SERVICE_URL=<target-url> ./scripts/release-validation/validate-cx-access-admin.sh v{VERSION}

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/cx-access-admin-validation-report.md
completion_criteria: Unauthenticated access to admin access endpoints is rejected (401/403)
Authenticated admin checks pass when ADMIN_BEARER_TOKEN is provided
Optional write-path smoke check passes when ALLOW_WRITE=true

tasks.name: Production Deployment Checklist Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST_v{VERSION}.md is complete and accurate.

**Actions:**
1. Check if docs/releases/v{VERSION}/PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST_v{VERSION}.md exists
2. If not, auto-generate from template
3. Validate checklist includes:
   - Pre-deployment tasks (backups, secrets rotation, DNS updates)
   - Deployment steps (sequential, executable commands)
   - Post-deployment validation steps (health checks, smoke tests)
   - Rollback procedures (with specific commands)
   - Communication plan (stakeholder notifications)
4. Verify all steps are testable and have clear success criteria

**Completion Criteria:**
- PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST_v{VERSION}.md exists
- Pre-deployment section complete
- Deployment steps clear and executable
- Post-deployment validation checklist present
- Rollback procedure documented with commands

**Auto-Generation:**
If file missing: ./scripts/release-validation/generate-release-docs.sh v{VERSION}

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST_v{VERSION}.md

phases.name: Final Release Preparation
phases.description: Execute final pre-release checks, create git tag, and prepare GitHub release

tasks.name: Version Matrix Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate VERSION_MATRIX_v{VERSION}.md contains accurate version numbers for all components.

**Actions:**
1. Check if docs/releases/v{VERSION}/VERSION_MATRIX_v{VERSION}.md exists
2. If not, auto-generate from template (extracts versions from gradle/libs.versions.toml, docker-compose.yml)
3. Validate content includes accurate versions for:
   - All 34 microservices (with port numbers)
   - Infrastructure components (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, Jaeger)
   - Dependencies (Spring Boot, HAPI FHIR, Liquibase, HikariCP)
   - Docker base images (openjdk, postgres, redis, kafka)
   - Compatibility matrix (Java, Gradle, Node.js versions)
4. Cross-reference versions with gradle/libs.versions.toml

**Completion Criteria:**
- VERSION_MATRIX_v{VERSION}.md exists
- All 34 service versions documented
- Infrastructure versions accurate
- Dependency versions match gradle/libs.versions.toml
- Compatibility matrix complete

**Auto-Generation:**
If file missing: ./scripts/release-validation/generate-release-docs.sh v{VERSION}

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-version-matrix.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/VERSION_MATRIX_v{VERSION}.md

tasks.name: Git Repository Status Validation
tasks.prompt:
Validate git repository is ready for release tagging.

**Actions:**
1. Run git status and verify:
   - No unstaged modified files (except docs/releases/)
   - No merge conflicts exist
   - Recent commits follow conventional commits (feat:, fix:, docs:)
   - Branch synchronized with remote (git fetch, compare)
   - No .gitignore violations
2. Verify no uncommitted experimental code or debug statements
3. Check that all validation reports are committed
4. Generate git status report

**Completion Criteria:**
- git status clean (no unstaged changes except docs/releases/)
- No merge conflicts
- Commits follow convention (feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:)
- Branch up-to-date with remote
- No .gitignore violations

**Validation Command:**
./scripts/release-validation/validate-git-status.sh

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/validation/git-status-report.md
completion_criteria: git status shows no unstaged modified files
Recent commits follow conventional commits format

tasks.name: Create Git Tag
tasks.prompt:
Create annotated git tag for release and push to remote.

**Actions:**
1. Create annotated git tag:
   git tag -a v{VERSION} -m "Release v{VERSION}: [brief summary from RELEASE_NOTES]"
2. Verify tag points to correct commit hash (git log --oneline -1)
3. Push tag to remote: git push origin v{VERSION}
4. Confirm tag appears on GitHub repository tags page
5. Generate tag confirmation report

**Completion Criteria:**
- Annotated git tag v{VERSION} created successfully
- Tag points to correct commit hash
- Tag pushed to remote repository
- Tag visible on GitHub tags page

**Commands:**
```bash
git tag -a v{VERSION} -m "Release v{VERSION}: [summary]"
git push origin v{VERSION}
```

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/git-tag-confirmation.md

tasks.name: Create GitHub Release
tasks.prompt:
Create GitHub release from tag with documentation attachments.

**Actions:**
1. Navigate to GitHub releases page: https://github.com/{ORG}/hdim/releases/new
2. Select tag: v{VERSION}
3. Set release title: "HDIM Platform v{VERSION}"
4. Copy content from RELEASE_NOTES_v{VERSION}.md to description
5. Attach documentation files:
   - UPGRADE_GUIDE_v{VERSION}.md
   - VERSION_MATRIX_v{VERSION}.md
   - PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST_v{VERSION}.md
   - KNOWN_ISSUES_v{VERSION}.md (if exists)
6. Mark as "Latest release" (if applicable)
7. Publish release
8. Save GitHub release URL

**Completion Criteria:**
- GitHub release created for v{VERSION}
- Release notes populated from RELEASE_NOTES
- All documentation files attached
- Release marked as "Latest" (if applicable)
- Release is publicly visible

**Expected Output:**
docs/releases/v{VERSION}/github-release-url.txt
completion_criteria: GitHub release created for tag v{VERSION}

ralph_loop_config.description: Configuration for executing each phase as a Ralph Wiggum loop
notes: Each phase runs as a separate ralph-loop invocation
User confirms before starting next phase (semi-automated approach)

phase_execution_pattern.phase_1: /ralph-loop "Execute Phase 1: Code Quality & Testing Validation. Complete tasks 01-04. Output <promise>PHASE_1_COMPLETE</promise> when all validation reports created and tests pass." --max-iterations 10 --completion-promise "PHASE_1_COMPLETE"
phase_execution_pattern.phase_2: /ralph-loop "Execute Phase 2: Documentation Completeness Validation. Complete tasks 05-08. Auto-generate missing docs. Output <promise>PHASE_2_COMPLETE</promise> when all documentation validated." --max-iterations 10 --completion-promise "PHASE_2_COMPLETE"
phase_execution_pattern.phase_3: /ralph-loop "Execute Phase 3: Integration & Configuration Validation. Complete tasks 09-12. Output <promise>PHASE_3_COMPLETE</promise> when all integration checks pass." --max-iterations 10 --completion-promise "PHASE_3_COMPLETE"
phase_execution_pattern.phase_4: /ralph-loop "Execute Phase 4: Deployment Readiness Validation. Complete tasks 13-16a and 16. Output <promise>PHASE_4_COMPLETE</promise> when deployment checks, CX access-admin validation, and checklist review are complete." --max-iterations 10 --completion-promise "PHASE_4_COMPLETE"
phase_execution_pattern.phase_5: /ralph-loop "Execute Phase 5: Final Release Preparation. Complete tasks 17-20. Output <promise>PHASE_5_COMPLETE</promise> when git tag created and GitHub release published." --max-iterations 10 --completion-promise "PHASE_5_COMPLETE"
